maps_to.constraints_text: ≥ ₹3 cr; calculated on restated, consolidated basis; each year has 12 months

source.span_hint: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees,

text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets.
notes: Net tangible assets requirement with a limit on monetary assets.
title: Net tangible assets ≥ ₹3 cr, with limit on monetary assets

source.span_hint: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees,

text: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years.
notes: Three-year operating profit requirement; computed on restated and consolidated basis.
title: Operating profit ≥ ₹15 cr in each of last 3 years

source.span_hint: it has a net worth

text: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis:
notes: Three-year net worth requirement; computed on restated and consolidated basis.
title: Net worth ≥ ₹1 cr in each of last 3 years

source.span_hint: it has made an application

text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX;
notes: Application for in-principle approval from stock exchange(s).
title: Application for in-principle approval

source.span_hint: it has entered into an agreement

text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;
notes: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation of specified securities.
title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held

text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;
notes: Promoters' specified securities must be in dematerialised form before offer document filing.
title: Dematerialised form of promoter's securities

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up

text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;
notes: Existing partly paid-up equity shares must be fully paid-up or forfeited.
title: Fully paid-up equity shares or forfeiture

source.span_hint: it has made firm arrangements

text: it has made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means towards seventy five per cent. of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from the issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the proposed public issue or through existing identifiable internal accruals.
notes: Firm arrangements of finance for 75% of the project's funding.
title: Firm arrangements of finance

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered...

text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document:
notes: Minimum holding period for equity shares offered for sale.
title: Minimum holding period for equity shares offered for sale

source.span_hint: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange

text: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.
notes: Holding period for convertible securities and resulting equity shares; calculation of 1 year period.
title: Holding period for convertible securities and resulting equity shares

source.span_hint: Provided that the amount raised...

text: Provided that the amount raised for such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer:
notes: Maximum percentage of funds raised that can be used for unidentified acquisition or investment targets.
title: Maximum sale of equity shares for unidentified acquisition or investment targets

source.span_hint: Provided further that such limits shall not apply if...

text: Provided further that such limits shall not apply if the proposed acquisition or strategic investment object has been identified and suitable specific disclosures about such acquisitions or investments are made in the draft offer document and the offer document at the time of filing of offer documents.
notes: Exceptions to the maximum sale limits for general corporate purposes and unidentified acquisition or investment targets.
title: Exceptions to maximum sale of equity shares for general corporate purposes and unidentified acquisition or investment targets

source.span_hint: Provided further that such specified securities...

text: Provided further that such specified securities being issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in the books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the financial year in which the draft offer document is filed with the Board; and such equity shares not being issued by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealized profits of the issuer.
notes: Additional conditions for conversion or exchange of compulsorily convertible securities.
title: Additional conditions for conversion or exchange of compulsorily convertible securities

source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments

text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments even without making a prior public issue of its equity shares and listing thereof. Provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.
notes: Eligibility for issuing convertible debt instruments without prior equity IPO.
title: Issuer may issue convertible debt instruments without prior equity IPO

source.span_hint: it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency

text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions: (a) it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency.
notes: Credit rating requirement for CDI offer.
title: Credit rating required for CDI offer

source.span_hint: Provided that it is not in default

text: Provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.
notes: Default payment status; Provided that clause.
title: Default payment of interest or repayment of principal

source.span_hint: it has obtained credit rating

text: it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency; it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993
notes: Credit rating and debenture trustee requirements.
title: Credit rating and debenture trustee requirements

source.span_hint: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve

text: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder
notes: Debenture redemption reserve requirement.
title: Debenture redemption reserve

source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments

text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.
notes: Requires positive consent from holders for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments.
title: Consent for conversion of convertible debt instruments

source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion

text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares.
notes: Gives holders of listed convertible debt instruments the option to not convert if conversion price is undetermined.
title: Option to not convert convertible portion into equity shares

source.span_hint: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;
notes: Warrant tenure limit.
title: Warrant tenure ≤ 18 months

source.span_hint: the price or formula for determination of exercise price

text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;
notes: Warrant exercise price formula and upfront payment.
title: Warrant exercise price formula and upfront payment

source.span_hint: in case the warrant holder does not exercise

text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.
notes: Forfeiture of consideration for un-exercised warrants.
title: Forfeiture of consideration for un-exercised warrants

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least

text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital:
notes: Minimum promoters’ contribution requirement.
title: Minimum promoters’ contribution ≥ 20%

source.span_hint: either by way of equity shares38,

text: either by way of equity shares38[, including SR equity shares held, if any,] or by way of subscription to convertible securities:
notes: Promoter contribution method.
title: Promoter contribution by equity shares or convertible securities

source.span_hint: if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant

text: if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.
notes: Promoter contribution in case of undisclosed conversion price.
title: Promoter contribution in case of undisclosed conversion price

source.span_hint: Explanation: For the purpose of this regulation:

text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this regulation:
(I)  Promoters’ contribution shall be computed on the basis of the post-issue expanded capital:
(a)  assuming full proposed conversion of convertible securities into equity shares;
(b)  assuming exercise of all vested options, where any employee stock options or stock appreciation rights are outstanding at the time of initial public offer in terms of proviso (a) to sub-regulation (2) of regulation 5.
(II)  For computation of “weighted average price”:
(a)  “weight” means the number of equity shares arising out of conversion of such specified securities into equity shares at various stages;
(b)  “price” means the price of equity shares on conversion arrived at after taking into account the predetermined conversion price at various stages.
notes: Computation rules for promoters' contribution and weighted average price.
title: Promoters' contribution computation

source.span_hint: For the computation of minimum promoters’ contribution,

notes: Securities not eligible for minimum promoters' contribution.
title: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution

source.span_hint: Promoters’ contribution shall be computed

text: Promoters’ contribution shall be computed on the basis of the post-issue expanded capital:
notes: Computation method for promoter's contribution.
title: Promoters’ contribution computation

source.span_hint: assuming exercise of all vested options

text: (b) assuming exercise of all vested options, where any employee stock options  39[or stock  appr...]
notes: Assumption made for exercising employee stock options or SARs.
title: Exercise of employee stock options or SARs

source.span_hint: The specified securities are offered in the initial public offer

text: The specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and had been acquired; The difference between the price at which the specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and the price at which the specified securities had been acquired is paid to the issuer.
notes: Initial public offer; payment for specified securities acquired by promoters.
title: Payment for specified securities acquired by promoters

source.span_hint: Specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme

text: Specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government; The promoters have invested business and capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval.
notes: Specified securities acquired under Companies Act; payment for specified securities.
title: Payment for specified securities acquired under Companies Act

source.span_hint: An initial public offer by a government company

text: An initial public offer by a government company, statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up by any of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector.
notes: Initial public offer for government company, statutory authority or corporation.
title: Payment for specified securities in government company IPO

source.span_hint: Specified securities allotted to the promoters

text: Specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India during the preceding one year at a price less than the issue price, in case of an issuer formed by conversion of one or more partnership firms or limited liability partnerships.
notes: Specified securities allotted to promoters at a price less than issue price.
title: Specified securities allotted to promoters at a price less than issue price

source.span_hint: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable

text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable (hereinafter referred to as “lock-in”) for the periods as stipulated hereunder:
notes: Lock-in periods for specified securities held by promoters.
title: Lock-in of specified securities held by the promoters

source.span_hint: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable

text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, except if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be three years.
notes: Lock-in period for promoter contribution; exception for capital expenditure.
title: Lock-in period for promoter contribution

source.span_hint: The promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution

text: The promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, except if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be one year.
notes: Lock-in period for promoter excess holding; exception for capital expenditure.
title: Lock-in period for promoter excess holding

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters

text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.
notes: Lock-in period for equity shares held by persons other than promoters.
title: Lock-in of equity shares held by persons other than promoters

source.span_hint: Provided that nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to:

text:
Provided that nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to: 
(a) equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme; (b) equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees by an employee stock option trust pursuant to exercise of options by the employees, whether currently employees or not, in accordance with the employee stock option plan or employee stock purchase scheme;
notes: Exceptions for equity shares held by employees and employee stock option trusts.
title: Exceptions to lock-in for equity shares held by persons other than promoters

source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I

text:
c) equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor: 
Provided that such equity shares shall be locked in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase by the venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of Category I or Category II or foreign venture capital investor.
notes: Lock-in period for equity shares held by venture capital funds or alternative investment funds.
title: Lock-in for equity shares held by venture capital funds or alternative investment funds

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16

text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;
notes: Pledge of locked-in specified securities allowed if loan granted to issuer or subsidiary for issue financing.
title: Conditions for pledge of locked-in specified securities (a)

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16

text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.
notes: Pledge of locked-in specified securities allowed if pledge is one of the loan terms.
title: Conditions for pledge of locked-in specified securities (b)

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged

text: Specified securities68[, except SR equity shares,] held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following:
notes: Specified securities locked-in by promoters can be pledged as collateral for loans; excludes SR equity shares.
title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition

text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition  of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities69[, except SR equity shares,] held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation  17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.
notes: Provisions for transfer of locked-in specified securities; subject to other SEBI regulations.
title: Transfer of locked-in specified securities

source.span_hint: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager,

text: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager, the rights, obligations and responsibilities, relating inter alia to disclosures, allotment, refund and underwriting obligations, if any, of each lead manager shall be predetermined and be disclosed in the draft offer document and the offer document as specified in Schedule I.
notes: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of multiple lead managers; predetermined and disclosed.
title: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of lead managers
